title: Skill Stacking

content.statement: Combining unrelated skills can create a unique advantage that competitors cannot easily copy.
content.question: What combination of skills gives you an advantage no one else can easily replicate?
content.equation: Unique Advantage = Skill A × Skill B × Skill C × Personal Context
content.mental_model: Custom recipe, the power comes from the combination, not one ingredient.
content.business_lens: Build offers and positioning around the rare combination of things you can do well.
content.life_lens: Stop seeing your varied interests as scattered and start arranging them into an advantage.
sop: Identify intersections competitors would struggle to copy
Build an offer, role, or brand around that intersection
Strengthen the weakest skill in the stack
content.explainer: Skill Stacking means your edge may not come from being the best in the world at one thing. It may come from combining several strong abilities in an unusual way. Your stack becomes your personal moat.
questions: What combination of skills gives you an advantage no one else can easily replicate?
Which two of your skills have you never actually combined into a single offer?
If a competitor tried to copy your exact combination of experience, what would they be missing?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Skill A 8, Skill B 6, Skill C 7, Personal Context 9. Score = 8 times 6 times 7 times 9 = 3,024 out of a possible 10,000. Higher means your specific combination is hard to hire away or copy. Lower means any one of your skills could be replaced by someone cheaper.
content.closing_statement: Your edge is the combination, not the skill.

caption:
Principle Nº 266: Skill Stacking

Combining unrelated skills can create a unique advantage that competitors cannot easily copy. Skill Stacking means your edge may not come from being the best in the world at one thing.

Ask yourself: What combination of skills gives you an advantage no one else can easily replicate?
